content.problemStatement:
Bhoomi was excited for the annual fest at **IIIT Pune**. She found a spot in the queue, eagerly waiting for the performance. But there was a problem: tall students ahead blocked her view!

Frustrated, she wondered: "How much do I have to move to be able to see the performance?"

There are NNN students who want to watch the performance at the fest. They're standing in a straight queue, and the iii-th student in the queue has a height of HiH_iHi‚Äã.

A student will be able to watch the performance if and only if they are **strictly taller** than every student ahead of them.

That is, student iii will be able to watch the performance if and only if Hi>HjH_i \gt H_jHi‚Äã>Hj‚Äã for every 1‚â§j<i1 \leq j \lt i1‚â§j<i.

Bhoomi is the last student in the queue, with a height of HNH_NHN‚Äã.

Every second, she can swap places with the person just in front of her - that is, if she's currently at position iii,

she can move to position i‚àí1i-1i‚àí1 (and the person previously at position i‚àí1i-1i‚àí1 will move to position iii instead).

How many seconds will it take till Bhoomi is able to see the performance?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of students in the queue.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \ldots, H_NH1‚Äã,H2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äã, denoting the heights of the students in the queue.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the number of seconds till Bhoomi is able to see the performance.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The process is as follows.

Initially, Bhoomi is at position N=6N = 6N=6 with a height of 333, and cannot see the performance - for example, H5=6H_5 = 6H5‚Äã=6 is greater than her height.
Second 111: Bhoomi moves to position 555, so the heights are now [2,4,1,7,3,5][2, 4, 1, 7, 3, 5][2,4,1,7,3,5]. Bhoomi still cannot see the performance.
Second 222: Bhoomi moves to position 444, so the heights are now [2,4,1,3,7,5][2, 4, 1, 3, 7, 5][2,4,1,3,7,5]. Bhoomi still cannot see the performance.
Second 333: Bhoomi moves to position 333, so the heights are now [2,4,3,1,7,5][2, 4, 3, 1, 7, 5][2,4,3,1,7,5]. Bhoomi still cannot see the performance.
Second 444: Bhoomi moves to position 222, so the heights are now [2,3,4,1,7,5][2, 3, 4, 1, 7, 5][2,3,4,1,7,5].
Bhoomi can now see the performance, so it took four seconds.

Test case 222: Bhoomi can see the performance from the start.
Test case 333: Bhoomi needs to move to the start of the queue to see the performance, which will take her three seconds.
